text: Integrate changing employment trends, societal needs, and economic conditions into career planning
justification: Societal needs referenced in career planning context. Does not require analysis of distributional consequences or community health equity. Coded Absent: career planning ≠ equity analysis.

text: Comply with safe handling/disposal per OSHA, EPA, HazCom, MSDS
justification: Environmental regulations (EPA) referenced but solely in occupational compliance context. Workers learn to follow disposal rules, not to analyze whether disposal patterns burden specific communities. Coded Absent: regulatory compliance ≠ equity analysis.

text: Explore issues of global significance and document impact on BCT sector
justification: Global issues explored but framed as industry impact analysis. Does not require distributional or community health perspective. Coded Absent: industry impact ≠ community equity.

text: Respect individual and cultural differences and recognize the importance of diversity in the workplace
justification: Diversity referenced in interpersonal workplace context. Does not address structural inequity in infrastructure siting, funding, or health outcomes. Coded Absent: workplace diversity ≠ environmental justice.

text: Understand environmental regulations (CEQA/EIR) that influence design
justification: CEQA/EIR are regulatory compliance processes that can include EJ analysis, but the standard requires understanding the regulations as compliance constraints, not evaluating distributional justice. Coded Absent: knowing regulations exist ≠ applying equity analysis.

text: Understand, integrate, and employ sustainable construction practices
justification: Sustainability standards address energy efficiency and material performance. None require analysis of who benefits from or is burdened by these choices. Coded Absent: technical sustainability ≠ environmental justice.

text: Work effectively and sustainably in the construction industry
justification: Sustainability scope = workplace waste reduction and efficiency. Does not require evaluating distributional consequences of construction on surrounding communities. Coded Absent: workplace sustainability ≠ community equity.

text: Know about environmental considerations in relation to construction
justification: Environmental considerations = energy efficiency, waste management, material selection. Does not address community health impacts or distributional justice. Coded Absent: environmental awareness ≠ environmental justice.
